faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Jackson Heights home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool, silk, or cashmere fabrics, silky tubes or cases in closets, adult moths flying near wardrobes, and webbing in fabric folds. You may also notice larvae (small white caterpillars) on clothing or damage to rugs and upholstery. Our Jackson Heights technicians can perform a thorough inspection to confirm the presence of clothes moths.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control service include in Jackson Heights?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a detailed inspection of all affected areas, identification of moth species (webbing or casemaking moths), treatment of infested materials using appropriate methods, application of targeted treatments to closets and storage areas, and detailed prevention recommendations. We also offer follow-up visits to ensure complete elimination and can treat entire apartments or focus on specific rooms depending on your needs.

faqs.question: Why does clothes moth control cost between $550 and $5,000?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on the size of your Jackson Heights property, severity of infestation, number of rooms requiring treatment, and whether multiple follow-up visits are needed. A single-room treatment in a small apartment may cost $550, while a severe infestation throughout a large multi-bedroom home requiring multiple treatments and specialized techniques can reach $5,000. We provide free estimates and clear pricing before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my Jackson Heights home?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regular vacuuming of closets and storage areas, storing woolens in airtight containers or garment bags, dry cleaning or washing items before long-term storage, maintaining low humidity levels, and regularly inspecting stored fabrics. Our technicians provide customized prevention plans for Jackson Heights homes and can recommend cedar products, moth repellents, and storage solutions specific to your situation.
